body:
This PR introduces our gaussian smoothing function and removes the tower of dependencies that comes with `ImageFiltering`. Removes lots of dependencies and reduces time to `using ClimaAtmos` by 10 %.

Our gaussian smoothing function is not precisely identical to `imfilter` (e.g., how we treat window sizes and edges), but results are nearly identical. 

![image](https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaAtmos.jl/assets/9167485/a246bc37-1b80-4596-9787-547c6f223df1)

Same, but with `imfilter

![image](https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaAtmos.jl/assets/9167485/2fc94802-b7b7-470c-a43f-3630c007a7ca)

Closes https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaAtmos.jl/issues/2323

Before (on 1.10)
```
julia> @time using ClimaAtmos
  4.011574 seconds (5.14 M allocations: 453.871 MiB, 12.37% gc time, 8.76% compilation time: 75% of which was recompilation)
```
After (on 1.10)
```
julia> @time using ClimaAtmos
  3.619062 seconds (4.67 M allocations: 420.374 MiB, 13.62% gc time, 9.51% compilation time: 75% of which was recompilation)
```
title: Add gaussian smoothing

body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
<!--- One sentence to describe the purpose of this PR, refer to any linked issues:
#14 -- this will link to issue 14
Closes #2 -- this will automatically close issue 2 on PR merge
-->
Adds a cloud model which specifies whether the cloud properties are calculated from grid-scale or quadratures. Currently this is only used for cloud fraction, but we can use it for liquid water and ice too. Also changes the radiation input to cloud fraction and in-cloud lwp and iwp.

Closes #2054
